target.title: fix(browser): notify agent when click triggers download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

- When a browser `click` triggers a file download, the `/act` response returned only `{ok: true}` — no download path or filename
- The agent had no way to know the file was saved, causing it to retry the click (see #93250)
- Root cause: `saveAs` is async and downloads can arrive after the action returns — the old code sampled immediately after the action completed, missing in-flight saves

Fixes #93250

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-session.ts:283-336` | Add `beginActionDownloadCaptureOnPage()` — per-action download capture with `drain()` + optional `graceMs` |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-session.ts:246-264` | Extract `saveUnmanagedDownload()` — returns `{suggestedFilename, savedPath}` instead of just a path |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.interactions.ts:1697-1707` | Add `actionDownloadGraceMs()` — 500ms grace for click/batch/evaluate, 0 for others |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.interactions.ts:1729-1774` | Wire capture + drain into `executeActViaPlaywright`, propagating `downloads` in result |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.act.ts:684-706` | Include `downloads` in `/act` JSON responses for click, clickCoords, batch, and evaluate |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-session.test.ts` | +5 test cases: multi-download capture, dispose safety, capture scoping, zero-grace, double-dispose |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: After a browser `click` triggers a download, the `/act` response now includes `{downloads: {count, recent: [{suggestedFilename, savedPath}]}}` so the agent can see where the file was saved.

**Real setup tested**: Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux x86_64, Google Chrome 143.0.7499.169, openclaw worktree at commit `094f951955`

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:
```bash
# 1. Start HTTP server with download test page
python3 -m http.server 19998 --directory /tmp/download-test &
sleep 1
curl --noproxy '*' -s -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}" http://127.0.0.1:19998/index.html

# 2. Start Chrome with CDP (remote debugging protocol)
google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=19222 --no-first-run --user-data-dir=/tmp/chrome-cdp &

# 3. E2E test: connect via CDP → navigate → click → capture download
node download-e2e-test.mjs
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
$ curl --noproxy '*' -s -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}" http://127.0.0.1:19998/index.html
HTTP 200

$ curl --noproxy '*' -s http://127.0.0.1:19222/json/version | node -e "process.stdin.on('data',d=>{console.log(JSON.parse(d).Browser)})"
Chrome/143.0.7499.169

=== E2E test: connect CDP → navigate → click → capture download ===
$ node download-e2e-test.mjs
Title: Download Test
✅ DOWNLOAD: {"suggestedFilename":"test-download.txt","savedPath":"/tmp/dl-e2e-test-download.txt"}
✅ Content: "Hello from PR 93307 download test!"
VERDICT: download notification path works — agent can see savedPath
```

**Observed result after the fix**:
- Clicking a download link in a real Chrome browser correctly saves the file to disk
- The file path and contents are verifiable — the agent can now receive this path in the `/act` response
- `beginActionDownloadCaptureOnPage()` correctly captures async downloads during/after an action with `drain({graceMs: 500})`

**What was not tested**: Cross-platform behavior (macOS, Windows) — this machine is Linux x86_64 only. The download capture logic is path-agnostic (`DEFAULT_DOWNLOAD_DIR`, `writeViaSiblingTempPath`) so platform differences are unlikely, but not verified.

## Tests and validation

All 20 `pw-session` tests pass (15 original + 5 new download capture tests):
- Concurrent downloads in one action — `captures multiple concurrent downloads within one action`
- Disposal preventing re-capture — `dispose prevents new downloads from being captured after disposal`
- Double-dispose safety — `double dispose is safe`
- Download scoping to most recent capture — `downloads are scoped to the most recent capture (at -1)`
- Zero graceMs immediate return — `graceMs=0 returns immediately when no download has arrived`

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-browser.config.ts \
  browser/src/browser/pw-session.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
#      Tests  20 passed (20)
```

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: sunlit-deng
